# Student Loan Forgiveness - Every Path to Relief [2026]
> Last updated: 2026-05-01

> Complete guide to student loan forgiveness programs: PSLF, IDR, Brunner test, bankruptcy discharge, and SAVE plan updates. 2026...

## About
studentloanforgive.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Student Loan Forgiveness Status (2024-2026) - What's Happening Now: https://studentloanforgive.org/biden-forgiveness-status.html
- Borrower Defense to Repayment - Discharge for School Fraud - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/borrower-defense.html
- The Brunner Test - Discharging Student Loans in Bankruptcy - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/brunner-test.html
- Closed School Student Loan Discharge - When Your College Shuts Down - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/closed-school-discharge.html
- Income-Driven Repayment (IDR) Forgiveness - What Happens After 20-25 Years - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/idr-forgiveness.html
- Student Loan Forgiveness - Every Path to Relief: https://studentloanforgive.org/
- Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) - Complete Guide - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/pslf-program.html
- The SAVE Plan - Newest Income-Driven Repayment Option - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/save-plan.html
- Teacher Loan Forgiveness - Up to $17,500 for Qualifying Educators - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/teacher-loan-forgiveness.html
- Total and Permanent Disability (TPD) Student Loan Discharge - Student Loan Forgiveness: https://studentloanforgive.org/total-disability-discharge.html
- Privacy Policy: https://studentloanforgive.org/privacy.html

## Contact
- Website: https://openbankruptcyproject.org
- Email: contact@openbankruptcyproject.org
- GitHub: https://github.com/openbankruptcyproject

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
